name: accela-mcp
description: MCP server for the Accela Construct API — search records, inspections, fees, documents, and more.
long_description:
Connects Claude Desktop to your Accela tenant. Search permits, look up parcels and contacts, list inspections, read fees and documents — all from chat.

## After installing

1. **Get your Accela app credentials.** On the [Accela Developer Portal](https://developer.accela.com), create an app and copy its App ID and App Secret. Use the redirect URI `http://localhost:8765/oauth/callback` (or pick your own and configure it on both sides).
2. **Fill in the Configuration tab below.** App ID, App Secret, and Redirect URI are required. Leave **Encryption Key** blank — the server generates one on first run and persists it locally.
3. **Log in from chat.** Once installed, ask Claude:  *“Log me into Accela.”* Claude will call the `accela_login` tool, open your browser to Accela's authorize page, capture the redirect, and save tokens.
4. **Restart Claude Desktop** so the rest of the Accela tools (records, inspections, fees, etc.) become visible.

## In-chat tools that don't need login

- `accela_auth_status` — check whether you're logged in and when your token expires.
- `accela_login` — start (or repeat) the OAuth login.

If an Accela API call later returns 401, the response includes a hint pointing back at `accela_login`.

app_id.title: Accela App ID
app_id.description: From your app on the Accela Developer Portal (https://developer.accela.com).

app_secret.title: Accela App Secret
app_secret.description: From the same app on the Accela Developer Portal. Keep this private.

redirect_uri.title: OAuth Redirect URI
redirect_uri.description: Must match the redirect URI registered on your Accela app. Defaults to a loopback URL on port 8765.

mcp_key.title: Encryption Key (advanced)
mcp_key.description: Fernet key used to encrypt the local token file. Leave blank — the server generates one on first run and persists it under your platform's user config directory.
